title: geum × meinshausenii
intro: Geum × meinshausenii, also known as the alpine avens, is a hybrid plant species within the geum genus, specifically a cross between Geum montanum and Geum reptans. The roots and flowers of Geum × meinshausenii have been used in traditional medicine, particularly in Europe, for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of consuming Geum × meinshausenii include potential antioxidant and antiviral properties, which may help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with respiratory and gastrointestinal issues. Herbal preparations of Geum × meinshausenii can be made by infusing the dried roots and flowers in hot water, or by using the plant's essential oil in topical applications, although it is essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
